_comment: Copy this file to config/allowlist.json and edit for your environment. hosts: machines the bot may SSH into (key becomes the Discord choice); optional identity_file (absolute path as seen inside the open-terminal container, e.g. /home/user/.ssh/some_key) authenticates that host with a dedicated key instead of open-terminal's default fleet-wide id_ed25519 -- use this for anything outside your normal trusted fleet, see README.md's 'SSH key architecture' section. categories: one object per slash command (linux, docker, pihole, proxmox, windows, fileserver); each entry is a check the command may run. sudo: prefix the command with sudo. mutating: require a Confirm/Cancel button press in Discord before running. A category built with buildCategoryCommand({ ..., readOnly: true }) in its src/commands/<name>.js file refuses to start if any of its checks are mutating: true -- see src/commands/router.js.
